design_evolution_notes.description: The primary design is the single rectangular microstrip patch antenna at 28 GHz with inset feed, copper conductors, Rogers RT5880 substrate, and Table 1 geometry.
design_evolution_notes.label: base proposed patch

design_evolution_notes.description: Later sections combine several of the proposed antennas into an array for beam-shaping / beam-steering and 3-sectorization, targeting approximately 120° directional coverage. This is treated as an extension rather than the canonical base antenna.
design_evolution_notes.label: beam-shaping array extension

design_evolution_notes.description: The paper explicitly states that the antenna was modeled and simulated in CST Microwave Studio; the VNA/DUT language in the results section is generic and does not clearly establish a separately fabricated measurement campaign.
design_evolution_notes.label: simulation-centric evaluation

identified_antennas: Proposed rectangular microstrip patch antenna for 28 GHz 5G mobile communications
Beam-shaping / beam-steering antenna array used for 3-sectorization and 120° directional coverage
Literature-referenced coplanar-fed / slot-coupled patch antenna used as background context
proposed_final_antenna_rationale: The canonical final/proposed antenna is the single rectangular patch described by Table 1, the abstract, and the main theory/results sections. It is the design whose geometry and performance are directly tabulated and repeatedly summarized. The array/beam-shaping material is a later application of multiple such antennas and is therefore treated as a secondary variant rather than the primary canonical geometry.
selected_design_rationale: The paper’s authoritative geometry description is Table 1 plus the abstract/conclusion: a rectangular microstrip patch antenna at 28 GHz with inset feed, copper conductors, and Rogers RT5880 substrate. These sources consistently describe one core proposed antenna and its performance. The beam-shaping/3-sector array appears later as an extension that combines several antennas, so it is not selected as the canonical dominant design.
selected_design_summary: Dominant design: a single rectangular, inset-fed microstrip patch antenna for 28 GHz 5G operation, built on Rogers RT5880 with copper patch/ground. A later beam-shaping/3-sector array variant is discussed, but it is secondary to the core proposed patch geometry summarized in Table 1.

unresolved_conflicts.description: The paper presents a core single rectangular patch design and later discusses beam-shaping with multiple antennas in an array. The array appears to be a secondary extension, but the text sometimes frames it as part of the proposed antenna family.
unresolved_conflicts.topic: Dominant geometry vs beam-shaping array variant

unresolved_conflicts.description: The paper explicitly says the antenna was designed, modeled, and simulated in CST Microwave Studio, but the results section uses VNA/DUT language without clearly identifying a distinct fabricated prototype or separate measured dataset.

The abstract compresses the antenna as 3.494 mm × 5.3 mm × 0.003 mm, while Table 1 separately lists patch dimensions plus h = 0.032 mm and th = 0.003 mm. Table 1 is preferred for full geometry detail.
unresolved_conflicts.topic: Geometry shorthand in abstract versus Table 1
